summary: Complete the lock's assembly declaration rows — RequirementRow set-scope facets + a member→requirement satisfies family — so the lock carries the whole assembly the gate needs

per.section: The lock and drift — one vocabulary

edit.description: Enrich the declaration-row family so the lock is a complete assembly source. `RequirementRow` gains the set-scope facets it currently drops — `count` / `unique` / `membership` / `degree` (its own doc flags these as owed: 'the set-scope bounds … are the SDK producer's to record'). Add a member→requirement `satisfies` family (a `SatisfiesRow` of member id + requirement key) beside kind/clause/requirement/assembly, so the requirement↔satisfies join the roster/coverage tiers need rides the lock, not a re-import. Thread the new columns/family through `write_into`, `declarations_from_doc`, and each row's `to_table`/`from_table` — absent columns omitted, so a bare-harness lock round-trips unchanged.

`collect_declarations` / `requirement_row` write the enriched requirement facets from the `AuthorLayer`'s `Requirement` (count/unique/membership/degree already on `compose::Requirement`), and a new satisfies-row producer folds each imported member's `satisfies` keys into the `[[declaration.satisfies]]` family. Transitional producer — still sourced from the temper.toml layer; migrating the producer to the SDK is out of scope (fence-side dogfood, state.md).

tests.asserts: the requirement rows carry count/unique/membership/degree and the satisfies family records each member's keys; a double import is byte-stable; a bare-harness lock still round-trips

acceptance: The lock's `[[declaration.requirement]]` rows carry the set-scope facets (count/unique/membership/degree) and a member-satisfies family records each satisfies key; `drift::read_declarations` round-trips them byte-stable; no gate behavior changes yet (the gate flip is MANIFEST-MACHINERY-RETIRE).
notes: Predecessor the MANIFEST bail asked for (inbox 2026-07-04): the retire mis-sequenced because the lock could not carry the whole assembly. Pure lock-vocabulary enrichment, disjoint from the retire (drift.rs/import.rs only; `read_declarations` is `pub`, test-driven, so the unread columns are not dead code). Producer stays on temper.toml; the SDK-side dogfood producer is the session's fence-side half.

summary: Flip the gate to read its assembly from the lock's declaration rows and retire the temper.toml/roster.toml/bindings.toml gate machinery

per.section: Decision: one authored assembly, no configuration dialect

edit.description: `gate()` sources requirements + satisfies + authority + reachability + edges from `drift::read_declarations(workspace)` — the committed-artifacts-plus-lock seam (`20-surface.md`) — instead of the temper.toml `AuthorLayer`'s assembly reads and `assembly_artifacts::load`/`merge_assembly`. Convert each `RequirementRow` to the `compose::Requirement` the roster/coverage/graph tiers already take (like `to_requirement`); the requirement↔satisfies join comes from the lock's satisfies family. Drop the `assembly_artifacts` import + call. Floor clauses keep composing from the embedded built-in floor — the compiled default program, not a manifest. 40-composition Decision rejects `temper.toml` and its `roster.toml`/`bindings.toml` decomposition as a second authoring surface.

Retire the `AuthorLayer`'s gate-facing assembly reads that only the now-flipped gate path consumed (the `[requirement.*]`/`[edge.*]`/authority/reachability accessors used as the gate's source, and the roster parse). Keep the accessors the transitional import producer (LOCK-ASSEMBLY-ROWS-COMPLETE) still calls to write the lock rows — binding is the spread in `expect`, carried as declaration rows, not composed from a manifest at gate time.

The `[role.*]` roster parse over temper.toml retires; the set-scope check logic (count/unique/membership over each requirement's satisfier set) survives, now fed the lock-sourced `compose::Requirement` set the gate assembles. If the parse was roster.rs's only remaining input, retire the file and fold the surviving check helpers into their caller.
Drop the `pub mod assembly_artifacts;` line for the deleted module (linear chain — no co-edit conflict).

tests.asserts: reconciled — the roster's set-scope predicates gate over the lock-sourced requirement set, no roster.toml/[role.*] surface
reconciled or retired — temper.toml is no longer the gate's assembly source (it survives only as the transitional lock producer's input)
reconciled or retired — no manifest-as-corpus remains on the gate path

acceptance: The gate reads its assembly (requirements/satisfies/authority/reachability/edges) from the lock's declaration rows, never `temper.toml`/`roster.toml`/`bindings.toml`; `assembly_artifacts.rs` is deleted; the `--harness` path stays green (import writes the lock rows, the gate reads them). The transitional import producer still reads temper.toml to WRITE the lock — its SDK migration is out of scope.
notes: Rewritten after the voluntary bail (inbox 2026-07-04). Now a clean gate-flip-plus-deletion because LOCK-ASSEMBLY-ROWS-COMPLETE lands the whole assembly on the lock. NB the retirement is gate-side only: the temper.toml→lock producer survives as the transitional dogfood source until the SDK produces it. contract/ byte-parity fixtures were the manifest tripwire — reconcile to the surviving lock/projection goldens.

summary: Retire KIND.md / PACKAGE.md parsing — a kind's schema is an SDK interface, a floor is an exported clause array; no kind/package file format

per.section: Decision: field typing lives in the SDK — there is no kind file format

edit.description: Retire the `KIND.md` header grammar (format/unit_shape/activation keys) and string-name identity resolution (15-kinds Decision: no kind file format; 'built-ins are a module, and identity is an import' rejects string-name identity + provider qualification). A kind's runtime residue is the five declaration facts read from the lock rows, not a parsed file.

The built-in floor no longer embeds/parses `packages/<name>/PACKAGE.md`; a floor is an exported clause array in `@temper/claude-code` (10-contracts) whose rows arrive via the seam, and the engine carries a compiled default program (50-distribution) for SDK-less checking.
The embedded built-in kind set becomes the compiled default program (declaration rows), not a walk over the `kinds/<provider>/<name>/KIND.md` tree.
Clause parsing off `PACKAGE.md` retires; clauses are compiled predicates arriving as declaration rows.
The KIND.md/PACKAGE.md document-carriage parse retires; the surface-language header grammar for kind/package files is gone.
The build-time embed no longer walks the `kinds/`/`packages/` `*.md` trees to embed KIND.md/PACKAGE.md; it embeds the compiled default program instead.

tests.asserts: reconciled — clauses come from compiled rows, not a parsed PACKAGE.md
reconciled or retired — no KIND.md/PACKAGE.md template parse remains
generic extraction driven by the five facts, no per-kind/file-format parse

acceptance: The engine parses no KIND.md or PACKAGE.md; kinds and floors are declaration rows (SDK-authored or the compiled default program); extraction is the generic algebra driven by the five facts.
notes: Terminal demolition of the file-format vehicle. Curated `kinds/`/`packages/` trees are fence-excluded human territory — the SESSION migrates/retires them after emit lands (fence-side moves are the session's hands). This entry retires only the engine-side parse.

summary: Collapse the four read traversals into one read verb — temper explain <target>

edit.description: Add `Command::Explain { target }` — one read verb over the graph `check` computes (20-surface Decision: one read verb). Resolve one positional `<target>` across the four namespaces (member / requirement / leaf-grain address / neighborhood) by the ruled mechanism (John 2026-07-04, inbox): exactly one hit ⇒ explain it; multiple hits ⇒ ERROR listing each match's qualified spelling (`member:foo`, `requirement:foo`, …) for the retry; a qualified prefix is always accepted as an explicit spelling. Never guesses, never silently prefers a namespace. Projections, never gates; exits zero; discloses coverage (names what it cannot see).
Re-home the four traversals (why/requirements/impact/context) under `explain`, dispatched by the resolved target species. Reuse the graph-walk helpers left in place by CLI-COLLAPSE.

Reframe as explain tests: each target species walks its corner of the one graph; a member-vs-requirement name collision errors with both qualified spellings; a qualified prefix resolves directly; coverage disclosure asserted.

tests.asserts: explain <target> resolves member/requirement/address/neighborhood; a name collision errors listing qualified spellings; qualified prefixes accepted; never gates; discloses coverage

acceptance: `temper explain <target>` answers all four read questions over data check computes, exits zero, discloses coverage; an ambiguous bare target errors with each match's qualified spelling; a qualified prefix resolves directly; the four separate verbs are gone.
notes: Fork resolved (John 2026-07-04, inbox): bare target + collision error — cheap in the common ~95% case, explicit on ambiguity (git's ref-disambiguation ethos). Never guesses, never silently prefers. Terminal leaf; blocks nothing. An optional one-line corpus encode of the collision mechanism into the 20-surface 'one read verb' Decision is a human delta, not plan's to write.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm packages, the engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org + @temper scope, marketplace publish, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end; channel 1 (SDK publish) also rides the SDK-primary foundation

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution, 'install is two placements').

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery'). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering it. Scoped `@temper` names per the resolved `(project-name)` fork. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (the SDK authoring-face manifest — channel 1's `temper` core + `@temper/claude-code` provider module).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`temper` + `@temper/claude-code`, floors as exported clause arrays); ch2 = engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human creds.
